data.text: I’ll use the HELIX build workflow because this is an execute-bead implementation pass. I’ll first read the relevant test and document UI code, then patch only the files needed and commit once verification is green.

The worktree already has unrelated skill-directory changes, so I’ll leave those untouched and stage only the document UI/test files I change. I’m also loading the frontend design skill now because the bead changes a Svelte UI surface.

The current spec already contains FEAT-008 cases, so I’m using those as the behavioral contract rather than inventing new tests. A shell glob tripped on the Svelte route path; I’ll reread those files with quoted paths.

I’ve got the shape now: the route currently renders sanitized markdown and has a raw textarea editor only. I’m going to replace that with a shared raw-markdown edit state, a radio-controlled editor mode, and link handling on the rendered markdown container.

I’m editing the document detail Svelte route and the existing Playwright spec. The UI change stays local to the document page: rendered links are handled in the markdown container, and the editor mode state remains in the parent component as raw markdown.

The tests now expect the legacy save case to enter Plain mode before using the textarea, which matches the new default WYSIWYG editor requirement. I’m going to run the frontend type check next to catch Svelte and TypeScript issues before trying Playwright.
The frontend dependencies are not installed in this worktree, so the check cannot find `svelte-kit`. I’ll install the frontend package dependencies locally, then rerun the same checks.
The Svelte type check is clean apart from two pre-existing warnings in unrelated bead components. I’m running the documents Playwright spec now, which is the bead’s named acceptance gate.

The new acceptance cases are passing, but two older document-list checks are failing because their `getByText('Vision')` selector also matches the path cell. I’m tightening those selectors to title cells only, then I’ll rerun the same spec.
The previous Playwright preview server kept port 4173 occupied after the failed run. I’ll identify and stop that local process, then rerun the documents spec.

Port 4173 is held by a different concurrent DDx worktree, not this one, so I’m not going to kill it. I’ll wait briefly for that run to release the shared Playwright port; if it stays occupied I’ll use an isolated config override rather than touching another attempt.

The shared 4173 port is still being used by another worktree, so I’m switching verification to an isolated preview port with a temporary Playwright config outside the repo. This keeps the committed config unchanged and avoids interfering with the other run.

The documents spec passes on an isolated preview port: all 15 tests, including US-081a and US-083a, are green. I’m going to format the touched frontend files, then rerun the type check and documents spec once more on the formatted code.

Type checking remains clean apart from the unrelated existing warnings. Since 4173 is still occupied elsewhere, I’ll run the final documents spec against an isolated Vite dev server on 4273 to avoid changing generated build artifacts again.

The lint errors are fixed without changing the behavior: navigation now uses SvelteKit `resolve`, and the sanitized `{@html}` insertions have targeted ESLint suppressions. I’m doing the final check and documents e2e run now.

All targeted verification is green: formatting check, ESLint on the touched files, Svelte check, and the documents Playwright spec. I’m staging only the Svelte document route and its e2e spec, leaving the unrelated skill-directory changes out of the commit.

Implemented and committed:

`9369b92c feat: add documents spa editor toggle [ddx-a308bd84]`

Verification passed:
- `bun run check`
- `bunx eslint` on touched files
- `bunx prettier --check` on touched files
- `e2e/documents.spec.ts`: 15 passed

Unrelated pre-existing skill-directory changes remain unstaged.
